meta.title: o1 Watch
meta.subtitle: The people behind OpenAI o1 — and what they're working on now
meta.note: Roster is the Foundational + Leadership tiers of OpenAI's o1 contributions page (28 people). Links and 'highlights' are curated notable work/posts, not a live social feed — refresh on request.

influence.headline: o1 created the reasoning-model category the whole industry now follows.
influence.summary: Released September 2024, OpenAI o1 (“Learning to Reason with LLMs”) was trained with large-scale reinforcement learning to produce a long internal chain-of-thought before answering. Its key result: accuracy scales with test-time ‘thinking’ compute — a new scaling axis beyond pretraining. It established RL-on-chain-of-thought and inference-time scaling as standard practice and triggered fast-follow reasoning models across the field.

benchmarks.name: AIME (math olympiad qualifier)
benchmarks.result: 74% single-sample, 83% with 64-sample consensus, 93% re-ranked — vs GPT-4o’s 12%

benchmarks.name: GPQA (PhD-level science)
benchmarks.result: First model to exceed PhD-level human accuracy in physics/biology/chemistry

ripple_effects: Spawned OpenAI’s own lineage: o1-mini → o3 / o3-mini → o4-mini
Triggered competing reasoning models: DeepSeek-R1, Google Gemini 2.5 ‘thinking’, Anthropic extended thinking, Alibaba QwQ/Qwen, xAI Grok
Shifted evaluation focus toward hard math/coding/science reasoning over chat quality
Popularized ‘test-time compute’ as a first-class scaling lever

sources.title: OpenAI — Learning to reason with LLMs

people.name: Jakub Pachocki
people.bio: Polish computer scientist and former competitive programmer, OpenAI's Chief Scientist (since May 2024, succeeding Ilya Sutskever). Joined in 2017, led OpenAI Five and GPT-4, and now sets research direction including the push toward reasoning models and an autonomous AI researcher.

highlights.title: The two people shaping OpenAI's research
highlights.note: MIT Tech Review profile of Pachocki and Mark Chen

highlights.title: Building a fully automated researcher
highlights.note: MIT Tech Review on the AI-researcher grand challenge he leads

people.name: Jerry Tworek
people.role: VP of Research — o1 overall lead
people.bio: Mathematician and former quant trader; VP of Research at OpenAI focused on teaching models to reason about science, math and programming. Led the o1 and o3 efforts and earlier worked on Codex (the basis for GitHub Copilot); departed OpenAI in early 2026.

highlights.title: A new way forward for LLMs
highlights.note: Fast Company profile on his o1 reasoning breakthrough

highlights.title: Personal site / blog
highlights.note: millionintegrals.com — his writing and research focus

people.name: Liam Fedus
people.role: VP of Research (post-training) — now co-founder, Periodic Labs
people.bio: ML researcher (PhD, Université de Montréal; ex-Google Brain) who led OpenAI's post-training team. In March 2025 left to co-found Periodic Labs, building AI foundation models for materials science and chemistry.

highlights.title: Departure announcement
highlights.note: His own X post announcing he's leaving OpenAI

people.name: Lukasz Kaiser
people.role: Research scientist — co-inventor of the Transformer
people.bio: Polish AI researcher and logician, co-author of 'Attention Is All You Need' (the Transformer) and creator of Tensor2Tensor/Trax. After ~8 years at Google Brain, joined OpenAI in 2021 and was a foundational contributor to the o1 series; shared the 2024 NEC C&C Prize with his Transformer co-authors.

highlights.title: What's Next for AI? (MAD Podcast)
highlights.note: Interview on reasoning models and the future of AI

people.name: Mark Chen
people.bio: OpenAI's Chief Research Officer (elevated March 2025) and former Jane Street quant; joined in 2018. Instrumental in DALL·E, Codex, GPT-4 and multimodal reasoning, and co-leads overall research direction; also coaches the USA IOI competitive-programming team.

highlights.title: Shaping OpenAI's research
highlights.note: MIT Tech Review profile with Pachocki

highlights.title: On models, poker and what's next

people.name: Szymon Sidor
people.bio: Technical Fellow and longtime research engineer; built the Rapid large-scale RL framework used for OpenAI Five (Dota) and was optimization vice-lead on GPT-4. Sam Altman has named him (with Pachocki) as someone 'OpenAI would not be OpenAI without.'

highlights.title: Jakub and Szymon
highlights.note: Sam Altman blog post praising Sidor and Pachocki

highlights.title: GPT-4 contributions
highlights.note: OpenAI credits for his optimization work

people.name: Wojciech Zaremba
people.bio: Polish computer scientist and OpenAI co-founder who has led efforts across robotics (the Rubik's-Cube-solving hand), Codex/GitHub Copilot, and the GPT models behind ChatGPT. Now focuses on AI resilience and safety.

people.name: Ahmed El-Kishky
people.bio: AI researcher at OpenAI working on teaching models to reason and use tools, starting with competitive programming via expert iteration and RL. Foundational contributor to o1; PhD from UIUC (Jiawei Han's group), with prior NLP/graph-ML work at Twitter.

highlights.title: Competitive Programming with Large Reasoning Models
On the o1 reasoning reveal
highlights.note: Thread tied to o1's public launch

highlights.title: How to cite the o-series
highlights.note: Canonical citation for the o-series models

people.name: Daniel Selsam
people.bio: Researcher at OpenAI spanning theorem proving, probabilistic programming, neural SAT (NeuroSAT), formal math and synthetic data. Founded and leads the IMO Grand Challenge (AI for an IMO gold medal); PhD from Stanford (2019). Foundational contributor to o1.

highlights.title: Future of Math & AI (with Terence Tao)

people.name: Francis Song
people.bio: H. Francis Song, MTS at OpenAI and foundational contributor to o1. A former physicist/neuroscientist turned ML researcher; at DeepMind worked on multi-agent reasoning, theory of mind, and deep RL (e.g. the Hanabi challenge, Machine Theory of Mind).

highlights.title: Machine Theory of Mind
highlights.note: Neural nets modeling other agents' mental states

people.name: Giambattista Parascandolo
people.bio: Research Fellow at OpenAI and foundational researcher on the 'strawberry' reasoning team behind o1, o3 and GPT-5 Thinking. PhD at the Max Planck Institute / ETH Zurich (Schölkopf & Hofmann) on out-of-distribution generalization; prior work at DeepMind and Google X.

people.name: Hongyu Ren
people.role: Research scientist — o1-mini / o3-mini lead (later Meta)
people.bio: Foundational contributor to o1 and a lead on o1-mini and o3-mini (also contributed to GPT-4o). PhD in CS at Stanford, BS from Peking University; research on enhancing LLM reasoning and generalization. Later moved to Meta's superintelligence effort.

people.name: Hunter Lightman
people.role: Member of Technical Staff — process supervision / verification
people.bio: MTS at OpenAI working on reasoning and verification. Lead author of 'Let's Verify Step by Step,' showing process supervision beats outcome supervision on math — foundational to o1. Released the PRM800K step-level feedback dataset.

highlights.title: On o1 and teaching models to reason
highlights.note: Talk with Noam Brown and Ilge Akkaya

people.name: Hyung Won Chung
people.role: Research scientist — scaling, instruction tuning, reasoning (later Meta)
people.bio: AI research scientist (ex-Google Brain, ex-OpenAI, now Meta Superintelligence Labs); PhD from MIT. At Google: T5X, PaLM, instruction fine-tuning (Flan). At OpenAI: foundational contributor to o1 and Deep Research, led Codex-mini training. Talks emphasize scaling and exponentially cheaper compute.

highlights.title: Stanford CS25: History of Transformer Architectures
highlights.note: Lecture on scaling as AI's driving force

people.name: Ilge Akkaya
people.bio: Researcher and team lead in OpenAI's LLM reasoning group, building the models behind o1 and o3. PhD in EECS from UC Berkeley (cyber-physical systems); earlier robotics work (the OpenAI robotic hand / Rubik's cube) and program synthesis.

highlights.title: On o1 and teaching models to reason
highlights.note: Panel with Noam Brown and Hunter Lightman

people.name: Ilya Sutskever
people.role: OpenAI co-founder & former Chief Scientist — CEO, Safe Superintelligence Inc.
people.bio: Co-founder and former Chief Scientist of OpenAI; foundational deep-learning researcher (AlexNet, seq2seq). In June 2024 co-founded and now leads Safe Superintelligence Inc. (SSI). PhD at Toronto under Geoffrey Hinton.

highlights.title: SSI mission
highlights.note: One goal, one product: safe superintelligence

people.name: Jason Wei
people.bio: AI research scientist (ex-Google Brain, ex-OpenAI, now Meta Superintelligence Labs) known for popularizing chain-of-thought prompting, instruction tuning (FLAN) and emergent abilities. At OpenAI worked on reasoning and agents, contributing to o1. Prolific, candid writer on X and his blog.

highlights.title: Asymmetry of verification & verifier's law
highlights.note: Blog: tasks easy to verify get conquered by RL

highlights.title: Stanford CS25: Intuitions on Language Models

people.name: Jonathan Gordon
people.bio: Research Scientist at OpenAI (since 2020), based in London, and an o1 contributor. PhD in ML at Cambridge (MLG); thesis on probabilistic meta-learning and the neural process family (ConvCNP, ICLR 2020 oral).

people.name: Karl Cobbe
people.bio: Research lead at OpenAI focused on mathematical reasoning and RL. Led GSM8K and 'Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems,' co-authored 'Let's Verify Step by Step,' and earlier pioneered RL generalization (CoinRun, Procgen). o1 contributor.

people.name: Kevin Yu
people.bio: Foundational contributor to OpenAI o1; per OpenAI's contribution pages also contributed to code capability and data infrastructure for GPT-4 and the deep research project. 'Kevin Yu' is a common name and no public profile could be confidently verified, so links are intentionally omitted.

people.name: Lukas Kondraciuk
people.bio: Łukasz Kondraciuk, training-infrastructure engineer at OpenAI (self-described 'Strawberry training infra lead'), specializing in HPC and distributed systems. Built distributed training infra for GPT-4, GPT-4V and GPT-4.5. ICPC silver medalist (University of Warsaw). o1 contributor.

highlights.title: On o1 authoring OpenAI code PRs
highlights.note: Noted o1 had solely authored PRs to the OpenAI codebase

people.name: Max Schwarzer
people.bio: RL researcher focused on scaling, sample efficiency and representation learning. PhD at Mila (Courville & Bellemare); prior stints at Google Brain/DeepMind and Apple (SPR, 'Reincarnating RL'). Senior figure in OpenAI post-training, contributing to o1 and the strong IOI results, before moving to Anthropic.

highlights.title: On o1's IOI result
highlights.note: Model matching strong human contestants under IOI conditions

people.name: Mostafa Rohaninejad
people.role: Research scientist — reasoning, RL, competitive programming
people.bio: Research scientist at OpenAI and foundational contributor to o1. Previously at Covariant and Berkeley AI Research (BAIR); interests in meta-learning and RL. A public voice on OpenAI's reasoning milestones, including the perfect 12/12 score at the 2025 ICPC World Finals.

highlights.title: OpenAI wins 2025 ICPC World Finals (12/12)
highlights.note: Perfect score that would have ranked first among human teams

people.name: Noam Brown
people.role: Research scientist — reasoning, multi-agent, test-time compute
people.bio: Research scientist at OpenAI and a key figure behind the o-series and the test-time/inference-compute scaling paradigm. Co-created superhuman poker AIs Libratus and Pluribus (CMU PhD) and the Diplomacy AI CICERO at Meta FAIR. Pluribus was a Science top-10 breakthrough of 2019.

highlights.title: Teaching LLMs to Reason (Sequoia podcast)
highlights.note: How o1 reasons via chain-of-thought and test-time compute

highlights.title: Why he joined OpenAI
highlights.note: From poker/Diplomacy to the o1 paradigm

people.name: Shengjia Zhao
people.role: Research lead — co-creator of ChatGPT & GPT-4o (later Meta MSL chief scientist)
people.bio: Foundational contributor to o1 and co-creator of ChatGPT, GPT-4 and GPT-4o; also led o1-mini. PhD in CS at Stanford (generative models, calibration, uncertainty). In July 2025 became chief scientist of Meta Superintelligence Labs.

highlights.title: Launching o1-mini
highlights.note: Cheap reasoning model ~70% AIME, 1650 Codeforces elo

highlights.title: Chief scientist of Meta Superintelligence Labs
highlights.note: His new role leading ASI research at Meta

people.name: Trapit Bansal
people.bio: Co-led OpenAI's RL-for-reasoning effort and was a foundational contributor to o1, helping kickstart the company's RL reasoning work. PhD at UMass Amherst (few-shot NLP via meta-learning); interned at OpenAI in 2017 on multi-agent RL. In 2025 moved to Meta Superintelligence Labs.

highlights.title: Meta hires key OpenAI reasoning researcher
highlights.note: TechCrunch on his o1 role and Meta move

people.name: Vineet Kosaraju
people.role: Member of Technical Staff — process supervision, math/reasoning
people.bio: MTS at OpenAI (since 2020) and foundational contributor to o1, working on process supervision and math reasoning ('Mathgen'). BS/MS in CS from Stanford (human motion/trajectory prediction in the Vision and Learning Lab); head TA for CS230.

people.name: Wenda Zhou
people.bio: Researcher at OpenAI and foundational contributor to o1, including its multimodal capabilities (o1 SOTA on MMMU). PhD in statistics from Columbia; previously at NYU's Center for Data Science and the Simons Foundation. Interests span high-dimensional statistics, compressed sensing and deep learning.

highlights.title: o1 is multimodal & SOTA on MMMU
